title: Bridge Essay: Parkinson disease psychosis: from phenomenology to neurobiological mechanisms.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent-arising** (Phase 6) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Dependent-arising to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Parkinson disease psychosis: from phenomenology to neurobiological mechanisms.** — Nat Rev Neurol. PMID: 38225264. DOI: 10.1038/s41582-023-00918-8. Parkinson disease (PD) psychosis (PDP) is aspectrum of illusions, hallucinations and delusions that are associated with PD throughout its disease course. Psychotic phenomena can manifest from the earliest stages of PD and might follow acontinuum from minor hallucinations to structured hallucinations and delusions.
